title: [InstCombine] Drop NSW when converting `shl X, BW - 1` back into mul

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on srem optimization; this tests urem with both operands as shl operations, demonstrating the generalization applies to unsigned remainder as well

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i16 @urem_shl_shl_with_different_shifts(i16 %X) {
#0:
  %BO0 = shl nuw i16 %X, 5
  %BO1 = shl nuw i16 %X, 3
  %r = urem i16 %BO0, %BO1
  ret i16 %r
}
=>
define i16 @urem_shl_shl_with_different_shifts(i16 %X) {
#0:
  ret i16 0
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary emphasizes BW-1 shift edge case for NSW dropping; this tests NSW preservation with smaller shift amounts where NSW can be maintained

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @srem_shl_shl_both_nsw_small_shift(i32 %X) {
#0:
  %BO0 = shl nsw i32 %X, 4
  %BO1 = shl nsw i32 %X, 2
  %r = srem i32 %BO0, %BO1
  ret i32 %r
}
=>
define i32 @srem_shl_shl_both_nsw_small_shift(i32 %X) {
#0:
  ret i32 0
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes shl being converted to mul; this tests the reverse where mul constant equals shl-derived constant, showing bidirectional matching

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i64 @srem_mul_shl_reverse_order(i64 %X) {
#0:
  %BO0 = mul nsw i64 %X, 32
  %BO1 = shl nsw i64 %X, 6
  %r = srem i64 %BO0, %BO1
  ret i64 %r
}
=>
define i64 @srem_mul_shl_reverse_order(i64 %X) {
#0:
  %r = shl nsw i64 %X, 5
  ret i64 %r
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
